free_text: Structure of 7Be. (II) The 6Li(p,p")6Li** and
4He(3He,p")6Li** reactions
- Tables; technical details
- Elastic
scattering of protons by Li6 (same data in Table 1 of
thesis)
(I) The 6Li(p,p')6Li* reaction
In set of measurements the total cross section
was determined by measuring at one angle the yield of
the 3.56 MeV de-excitation gamma-rays to the 6Li
ground state.
Compilation produced by Arzamas RFNC-VNIIEF
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
Subent 002 was split in two.
SD: The digitized data were replaced with
tabulated data from thesis. Corrections in all Subents.
Subent 006 added.
At 5.80-MeV incident proton lab energy both the direct
measurement and the coincidence data were used in
calculations.
7Be center-of-momentum energy.

Table 6. Similar
data presented on fig.1 of NP/A,92,260,1967
Angular distributions.
The coefficient of P0 (=the total c.m.
cross section divided by 4Pi).
SD: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
STATUS updated. EN-CM -> E-EXC-CMP; EN-ERR -> E-ERR-DIG
Part of data moved to Subent 005. Data were redigitized
SD: BIB updated. The digitized data were
replaced with tabulated data from thesis.
The 6Li targets used ranged in thickness from about
30 to 130 ug/cm2. The lithium target thickness
traversed by the beam was about 50 ug/cm2 in gamma-ray
measurements.
The measurements were made at different times with
different targets, although the procedures followed
were quite similar.
The normalization of the angular distribution
was determined from an angular distribution obtained
later at the same energy by the direct measurement
technique, This distribution in turn was normalized
to the 6Li,(p,el)6Li cross section.
The gamma-rays were observed with a Harshaw 10.16-cm
long by 10.16-cm diameter cylindrical Nal(T1) crystal
mounted on a CBS 7819 photomultiplier tube.
Detector was used to monitor target thickness
and beam integration.
One of the particle
detectors used in the study of the 6Li(p,inl)6Li*
reaction (see ref.) was used to monitor target
thickness and beam integration. This was done by
comparing the proton yield from the 6Li(p,el)6L1
reaction with the previously determined cross
section see ref.).
The energy scale given was corrected for target
thickness.
Measurement in coincidence
Direct measurement
At 5.80 MeV the data from the coincidence
measurements and from the direct measurements are
given separately (see FLAG).
The greater part of the standard error given
for each point was estimated from the statistics of
the number of coincidences obtained, and the smaller
part from uncertainty in the correction for
random coincidences.
Error in the normalization relative to
the correct 6Li(p,el)6Li cross section.

Gas target 4He. The gas pressure was 8 cm of mercury.
Tantalum was used for the target tube in order to
reduce background radiation produced by particles
striking its walls.
Negatively charged 3He ions
The standard error given for each point was
estimated from a combined statistical and background
error.
The total normalization standard error.

P1-P4 coeffs.
SD: Part of Subent 002.
SD: BIB updated. The digitized data were
replaced with tabulated data from thesis.
Targets of 6Li (isotopic abundance
99%) with the thickness from 70 to 300 ug/cm2 were
prepared by evaporation in situ on 500 Angstr. thick
nickel foils.
These were mounted over 7.9-mm diameter holes punched
in 0.18-mm thick tantalum.
Two silicon surface-barrier detectors, each
with a resolution of about 40 to 50 keV, were used.
One at a fixed angle as a target monitor.
The estimated normalization standard errors
are those associated with the normalization of these
data to the angular distribution data at 5.80 MeV
obtained from particle measurements.
